stem: A clinical research study measures treatment response rate across 4 trial sites, each containing multiple patient cohorts. trial site A has 20 observations with mean 70.5 % (within-trial site SD = 3.3). trial site B has 5 observations with mean 75.2 % (within-trial site SD = 5.9). trial site C has 20 observations with mean 57.5 % (within-trial site SD = 7.0). trial site D has 15 observations with mean 61.4 % (within-trial site SD = 5.5). The between-trial site standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 8.2 and 20.5 %. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true treatment response rate for trial site C, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-trial site variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for trial site C: 57.6 % (shrunk from raw 57.5). Range [57.5, 57.7] as between-trial site SD varies from 8.2 to 20.5. Pooled mean: 64.3.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 64.3 % (weighted average of 4 trial site means).
Shrinkage factor for trial site C: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/20). At σ_between=13.7: B = 0.987.
Interval [57.5, 57.7] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (57.5) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (64.3) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
